steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what function the underlined sentence serves. The key structural clue is the word But that immediately follows it — a contrast signal indicating Arnold is about to oppose whatever the sentence just stated.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It suggests that opinions regarding culture change over time.: The "But" following the sentence signals opposition, not agreement. ✗
  • Choice B — It asserts that the English are not as well known for their sense of taste as they ought to be.: Arnold's view comes after "But"; the underlined sentence presents The Times' view, not his. ✗
  • Choice C — It details an example that supports the author's primary claim.: The sentence is the opposing position Arnold pushes back against, not supporting evidence for his side. ✗
  • Choice D — It presents an opinion with which the author disagrees.: The underlined sentence states The Times' view, and "But" signals Arnold's rejection of it. ✓
That is the correct answer.
